tasks.title: Create inbox_events database migration
tasks.description: Create Supabase migration `supabase/migrations/020_inbox_events.sql` with the inbox_events table, partial indexes on (agent_id, created_at) WHERE acknowledged = false, and (agent_id, type) WHERE acknowledged = false. Event IDs use `evt_` prefix.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: Table inbox_events created with columns: id (TEXT PK), agent_id (UUID FK), type (VARCHAR 64), payload (JSONB), acknowledged (BOOLEAN), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ)
AC-2: Partial index idx_inbox_events_agent_pending on (agent_id, created_at ASC) WHERE acknowledged = false
AC-3: Partial index idx_inbox_events_agent_type on (agent_id, type) WHERE acknowledged = false
GATE: Migration applies cleanly on fresh DB

tasks.title: Create insertInboxEvent helper function
tasks.description: Create `src/lib/inbox/insert-event.ts` with a reusable helper that inserts an event into inbox_events. Used by all side-effect tasks. Fire-and-forget within existing transactions.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: Function accepts supabase client, agentId, type, and payload
AC-2: Inserts into inbox_events with correct column mapping
GATE: TypeScript compiles without errors

tasks.title: Implement GET /api/agents/me/inbox endpoint
tasks.description: Create `src/app/api/agents/me/inbox/route.ts` with GET handler. Supports cursor (base64 opaque), types filter (comma-separated), and limit (default 50, max 100). Returns events ordered by created_at ASC with has_more and cursor pagination.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: Returns only unacknowledged events for the authenticated agent
AC-2: Cursor pagination works — passing cursor from previous response returns only newer events
AC-3: types filter correctly filters by comma-separated event types
AC-4: has_more is true when there are more events beyond the limit
AC-5: Invalid cursor returns 400 INVALID_CURSOR
GATE: curl to endpoint returns correct JSON shape

tasks.title: Implement POST /api/agents/me/inbox/ack endpoint
tasks.description: Create `src/app/api/agents/me/inbox/ack/route.ts` with POST handler. Accepts event_ids array, validates ownership, marks as acknowledged. Idempotent for already-acknowledged events.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: Marks events as acknowledged — they no longer appear in GET /inbox
AC-2: Returns 400 MISSING_EVENT_IDS if event_ids is empty
AC-3: Returns 404 EVENT_NOT_FOUND for non-existent event IDs
AC-4: Returns 403 AUTHZ_FORBIDDEN for events belonging to another agent
AC-5: Idempotent — acknowledging already-acknowledged events succeeds silently
GATE: curl to endpoint returns { acknowledged: N }

tasks.title: Side-effect: apply inserts application_received event
tasks.description: Modify `src/app/api/jobs/[id]/apply/route.ts` to insert an `application_received` event for the job poster after a successful application. Payload includes job_id, application_id, applicant_agent_id, applicant_name, proposed_price.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: After successful apply, inbox_events row exists for poster with type application_received
AC-2: Payload contains job_id, application_id, applicant_agent_id, applicant_name, proposed_price
GATE: Existing apply tests still pass

tasks.title: Side-effect: hire inserts application_accepted and application_rejected events
tasks.description: Modify `src/app/api/jobs/[id]/hire/route.ts` to insert `application_accepted` for the hired agent and `application_rejected` for all other applicants after a successful hire. Fetch rejected applicants before the atomic RPC changes their status.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: After hire, hired agent gets application_accepted event with contract_id and contract_status
AC-2: All other pending applicants get application_rejected events
AC-3: Rejected applicants fetched before RPC to capture correct state
GATE: Existing hire tests still pass

tasks.title: Side-effects: approve, complete, and rate insert inbox events
tasks.description: Three small changes: (1) approve route inserts contract_active for both agents, (2) complete route inserts contract_completed for hiring agent, (3) rate route inserts contract_rated for hired agent. Each is 3-5 lines of code.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: Approve inserts contract_active for both hiring_agent_id and hired_agent_id
AC-2: Complete inserts contract_completed for hiring_agent_id with proof_summary (first 200 chars)
AC-3: Rate inserts contract_rated for hired_agent_id with rating value
AC-4: Approve route SELECT includes hired_agent_id (add to existing select)
GATE: Existing tests still pass

tasks.title: Create cron purge-inbox endpoint
tasks.description: Create `src/app/api/cron/purge-inbox/route.ts` that deletes acknowledged events older than 7 days. Protected with CRON_SECRET. Add cron config to vercel.json (create if needed).
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: DELETE FROM inbox_events WHERE acknowledged = true AND created_at < NOW() - 7 days
AC-2: Endpoint validates CRON_SECRET authorization header
AC-3: vercel.json includes cron entry for /api/cron/purge-inbox at 0 3 * * *
GATE: Endpoint returns purge count on success

tasks.title: Critical unit tests for inbox endpoints
tasks.description: Write critical-path integration tests for GET /inbox and POST /inbox/ack. Cover: basic retrieval, cursor pagination, types filter, acknowledge flow, ownership isolation, error cases (invalid cursor, foreign events).
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: Test GET /inbox returns only unacknowledged events for authenticated agent
AC-2: Test cursor pagination returns only events after cursor
AC-3: Test types filter correctly narrows results
AC-4: Test POST /ack marks events as acknowledged
AC-5: Test ownership isolation (agent A cannot see agent B events)
AC-6: Test error cases: invalid cursor, missing event_ids, foreign event
GATE: All tests pass

tasks.title: Update 7 skill docs with inbox heartbeat
tasks.description: Apply all skill doc changes from the technical spec: index.md (new section), employer.md (state machine, Steps 4-5, inbox reference), employer-rules.md (inbox rules), employer-runbook.md (heartbeat rhythm), worker.md (state machine, Step 4, inbox reference), worker-rules.md (inbox rules), worker-runbook.md (heartbeat rhythm).
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: index.md has new 'Staying informed — the Inbox' section
AC-2: employer.md Steps 4-5 rewritten to use inbox; inbox reference section added; persistence format includes inbox_cursor
AC-3: employer-rules.md has Inbox rules section after Security rules
AC-4: employer-runbook.md renamed to Heartbeat rhythm; minimal loop steps 2 and 6 updated
AC-5: worker.md Step 4 rewritten to use inbox; inbox reference section added; persistence format includes inbox_cursor
AC-6: worker-rules.md has Inbox rules section after Security rules
AC-7: worker-runbook.md renamed to Heartbeat rhythm; minimal loop steps 4, 5, 6 updated
GATE: All {{BASE_URL}} placeholders preserved correctly

tasks.title: Code Review
tasks.description: Run meli-code-reviewer skill on all modified files. Verify patterns, naming, error handling, and consistency with existing codebase conventions.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: All findings resolved or acknowledged
GATE: meli-code-reviewer verdict passes

tasks.title: Performance Review
tasks.description: Run meli-performance-expert skill. Verify inbox query uses partial indexes efficiently, cursor pagination avoids full table scans, no N+1 in side-effect inserts.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: Inbox GET query plan confirms index usage
AC-2: No N+1 query patterns in side-effects
GATE: meli-performance-expert verdict passes

tasks.title: Security Review
tasks.description: Run meli-security-expert skill with ApplicationSecurityMCP. Verify agent ownership isolation, no data leakage between agents, CRON_SECRET validation on purge endpoint.
acceptance_criteria: AC-1: Agent A cannot read/ack agent B events
AC-2: CRON_SECRET properly validated
AC-3: ApplicationSecurityMCP list_security_issues called
GATE: meli-security-expert verdict passes
